164bf35e-192d-460e-95d8-abc47bd0a546.exp: Von Willebrand disease is inherited in an autosomal dominant fashion affecting both boys and girls. It is associated with prolonged APTT and normal PT and children presents with bleeding into joints. Therefore, VonWillebrand disease is the best choice. vWF is an unusually large multimeric glycoprotein that binds to platelet glycoprotein Ib receptor, bridge platelets together and attach them to the subendothelial matrix at the site of vascular injury. It also has a binding site for factor VIII. It is found to be defective or deficient in von Willebrand disease. Ref: In a Page Emergency Medicine By Jeffrey M. Caterino, Pages 62-3; CURRENT Medical Diagnosis and Treatment, 2012, Chapter 14

1eaf9e25-f299-4798-9a00-adbeca6d82ff.exp: The corpus striatum, which is paially divided by the internal capsule into two nuclei: (a) The caudate nucleus; and (b) the lentiform nucleus. These two nuclei are interconnected by a few bands of grey matter below the anterior limb of the internal capsule. The bands give it a striped appearance, hence the name.The lentiform nucleus is divided into a lateral pa, the putamen, and a medial pa, the globus pallidus. The caudate nucleus and putamen (neostriatum) are often grouped as the striatum, whereas the globus pallidus (paleostriatum) is the pallidum.The amygdaloid body forms a pa of the limbic system.Ref: BD Chaurasia; Volume 3; 6th edition; Brain; Page no: 358

e59c3124-8783-459c-b450-9aea6378b1cd.exp: Phenylephrine Human penis is made up of two spongy structures i.e., - Corpora cavernosa - Corpora spongiosum These structures contain distensile channels that become filled with blood when the aerial circulation is increased. The enlargement of the aerial channels presses on the veins situated in these structures preventing outflow of blood through them. The collection of blood in the distensile channels due to aerial dilatation leads to penile erection. Erection is mediated by parasympathetic nerves (S2, S3, S4) and non adrenergic and noncholinergic pathways which induces release of NO from endothelium in penile vasculature. NO induces vascular smooth muscle relaxation allowing enhancement of blood flow leading to erection. The primary drugs used in erectile dysfunction e.g. Slidenafil, Tadalafil and Vardenfil act on Nitric oxide pathway to improve erectile dysfunction. Nitric oxide mediates its action by increasing cGMP concentration. cGMP is finally degraded by an enzyme phosphodiesterase type 5 (PDE-5). Slidenafil and other oral agents are inhibitors of phosphodiesterase enzyme (PDE-5). These enzymes inhibits PDE-5 and thus they reduce the degradation of cGMP. Increased cGMP facilitates the relaxation of distensile channels of penis. Besides phosphodiesterase inhibitors several other drugs are also used these days - Prostaglandin Ei. E2 - Alprostadil - Transurethral alprostadil - Injectable phentolamine - Apomorphine (used in Europe only) PQK - Intracavernous injection PGE1 is widely used for treating erectile dysfunction. - PGE2 (intrauretbrall) can also be used to increase penile blood flow and treat erectile dysfunction. It relaxes human corpus cavernosum. Phenvlephrine - It is dung of choice for priapism

Examples are :S - SulfonamidesH- Hydralazine I -Isoniazid P-PASAcetylation is a genetically mediated condition.Multiple genes control the acetyl transferases and the rate of acetylation shows genetic polymorphism

92ca52b9-4989-4be7-8274-f9623fad6899.exp: Tetracyclines may cause staining of fetal teeth when given during pregnancy. Trimethoprim sulfamethoxazole may cause neonatal jaundice when administered late in pregnancy because sulfonamides may displace bilirubin from plasma proteins. Fluoroquinolones, such as ciprofloxacin and norfloxacin, are best avoided in pregnancy because of their potential to cause arthropathy in the fetus caused by damage to cartilage. The 13-lactam antibiotics, including penicillin's such as ampicillin and various oral cephalosporin's, may be safely administered to pregnant women. First- or second-generation cephalosporins are usually preferred because of their antimicrobial spectrum.

cd6615d2-34b6-4eb2-9342-c89586532f70.exp: This is varicella (chicken pox), which is the primary form of infection by the herpes zoster (varicella-zoster) virus. Recurrence due to virus harbored in neurons tends to be dermatomal in distribution and is called shingles. Fever, malaise, headache, and myalgia may also be present, paicularly in the prodromal phase. Tzanck smear of the base of a vesicle may demonstrate multinucleated giant cells. Immunocompromised patients can be treated with acyclovir to prevent dissemination. Chicken pox may be complicated by secondary bacterial infection, pneumonia, systemic spread (immunosuppressed patients), neurologic involvement (rare), Reye's syndrome (rare), and hemolytic anemia (rare). Herpes simplex I causes oral vesicles and ulcers. Herpes simplex II causes genital vesicles and ulcers. Measles causes a blotchy, non vesicular rash. Ref: Ray C.G., Ryan K.J. (2010). Chapter 14. Herpesviruses. In C.G. Ray, K.J. Ryan (Eds), Sherris Medical Microbiology, 5e.

2b0d4e5b-5b86-428a-ac68-e095396ce403.exp: Medical treatment of alcohol dependence involves administration of drugs such as Disulfiram, Naltrexone and Acamprosate. Flumazenil is a benzodiazepine antagonist and has no role in the treatment of alcohol dependence. Disulfiram is used as an aversive drug to discourage alcohol use. It acts by inhibiting alcohol dehydrogenase, resulting in toxic reactions when alcohol is consumed. Naltrexone an opiate antagonist, is useful in lowering relapse rates over the 3-6 months after cessation of drinking. It works by lessening the pleasurable effects of alcohol. Naltrexone is FDA-approved for maintenance therapy. Acamprosate is used to reduce craving and maintain abstinence, and can be continued even during periods of relapse. Ref: Current Medical Diagnosis and Treatment 2012, chapter 25.

ea0f0bb4-92de-4965-973b-5f01ab3ddd38.exp: Chalcosis refers to the specific changes produced by the alloy of copper in the eye. Copper from the alloy are dissociated electrolytically and are deposited under the membranous structure of the eye. Clinical manifestations of chalcosis includes: Kayser Fleischer rings: due to deposition of copper under peripheral pas of Descement's membrane. Sunflower cataract: due to deposition of copper under the posterior capsule of the lens. Deposition of golden plaques at the posterior pole of retina which reflects the light with a metallic sheen. Ref: Comprehensive Ophthalmology By AK Khurana, 4th edn, page 410

cb17d33d-fbb7-4e3c-bf76-1435ca43b623.exp: Ans. is 'a' i.e., Found in lower one - third of esophagus at squamocolumnar junction Schatzki's Ring* It occurs at the junction of squamous and columnar epithelium at the lower end of oesophagus and has also been called lower oesophageal ring.* Usually seen in patients above 50 years of age.* Cause is unknown.* Symptomatic patients complain of intermittent dysphagia and some may even present with bolus obstruction.* It may be associated with hiatus hernia.* Treatment is oesophageal dilatation.

f3e0136d-a734-45b6-a73c-35cf6a31327f.exp: The use-effectiveness of contraceptive method is expressed as Pearl rate & life table technique. Pearl rate or pearl index was introduced by Raymond Pearl in 1932 The rate is expressed as pregnancy rate per 100 women years (HWY) & calculated as Pregnancy rate per HWY = Total accidental pregnancies x 1200/ Number of women observed x Total months of use of contraceptive 1200 is the number of months in 100 years
f3e0136d-a734-45b6-a73c-35cf6a31327f.hint_exp: 🧩 Your crush solved "Pearl index formula Pregnancy" like a puzzle — methodically, confidently, and with a smile. That's your approach too. Piece by piece. Always right.
